Definition.text: Critical Strike is a hit classification that can apply an increased damage multiplier and optional status effects, producing a stronger result than a normal hit when triggered.

Purpose.text: Increase expected damage output for qualifying hits via the weapon/spell critical strike framework.

Core Rules.text: A hit can be critical only when the attack or spell hit succeeds; critical chance is then resolved by the hit event context and chance sources for that source.

Internal Rules.text: Critical Strike modifiers apply in addition to base hit rules and do not alter Hit itself; they alter critical outcome and resulting damage behavior.

Calculation Order.text: 1) hit is resolved 2) if hit is successful, roll critical chance 3) if critical succeeds, apply critical damage multiplier and critical-hit side effects.

Priority Rules.text: Increases to critical chance stack through standard increase/reduction and multiplicative modifiers; explicit overriding effects in the critical system follow the specific rule order above generic modifiers.

Preconditions.text: A source must have a successful hit opportunity; criticals do not occur from non-hit events.

Postconditions.text: On success, damage and related effects are evaluated using critical multipliers and any critical-specific conditional interactions.

Limitations.text: Critical chance is clamped by the engine model used for the source type; sources immune to critical behavior do not generate critical outcomes.

Caps.text: Critical chance is bounded by engine-defined minimum/maximum values for the source context; exact values are maintained per-source in patch data.

Formula.text: No single universal Critical Strike formula is valid for all sources; each source uses its own base critical chance, then applies chance modifiers.

Conflicts.text: Source-specific mechanics that replace hit classification can block or bypass the critical branch for their own resolution.

Exceptions.text: Critical interactions that grant guaranteed critical behavior are evaluated in the same branch after hit validation and are not guaranteed from chance modifiers.

Hidden Rules.text: Critical Strike uses distinct data fields per skill type and can be affected by conversion or conversion-like support rules in explicit support wording.

Developer Notes.text: Store Critical Strike separately from Hit and Damage so downstream systems can trace miss, critical, and multiplier stages independently.

Historical Changes.text: Global critical behavior tuning appears across multiple balance patches; per-patch details must be kept in linked patch history entries when extracted from official notes.

text: An attack with critical chance and multiplier applies normal hit effects first, then upgrades to critical when the critical roll succeeds.
Critical Strike chance is not the same as chance-to-hit.
Critical Strike is not always guaranteed by the presence of Critical Strike Chance on a source.

overrides: non-critical branch within the same hit event
removes: normal crit-uncertain outcome when rolled critical
